title: uidmap/userns can't be used with krun
body:
### Issue Description

`uidmap` or `userns=auto` won't work wiht krun, might related to the handling of switching into the container uid namespace?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ podman --runtime=crun run -it --rm --uidmap 0:100011:1024 fedora:latest bash
[root@3b08a295349b /]# cat /proc/self/uid_map 
         0     100011       1024

$ podman --runtime=krun run -it --rm --uidmap 0:100011:1024 fedora:latest bash
Error: krun: create directory `/run/user/1000/crun`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

# podman --runtime=krun run -it --rm --uidmap 0:100011:1024 fedora:latest bash
Error: krun: open `/run/crun/26bb1b359d6988746a75dc0e2d24c7751b774f081f18866ed6fc627e2d2e237b/config.json`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

```


### Describe the results you received

see above, error happes both rootful and rootless

### Describe the results you expected

I expected that krun is more or less a drop-in replacement in most case.

### podman info output

comments.body: I will start looking into it this week, thanks!

I believe the error is triggered in
`libcrun_container_notify_handler -> libkrun_configure_container`
during the `HANDLER_CONFIGURE_BEFORE_MOUNTS` phase.

https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/e0a6f43ffd97874abf95e9df3518585439e6beda/src/libcrun/container.c#L1339-L1341

Within `libkrun_configure_container()`, the code calls
`libcrun_get_state_directory() -> get_run_directory()`, which attempts
to open or create `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` for non-root users. The subsequent
calls to `read_all_file()`, `safe_openat()`, and `safe_write()` require
access to the configuration file.

https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/e0a6f43ffd97874abf95e9df3518585439e6beda/src/libcrun/handlers/krun.c#L470-L499

At this point, however, the process has already entered the container
UID namespace and therefore no longer has the required permissions to
access these paths.

This suggests either a bug in crun/krun or a design issue: specifically,
the handler may need to be invoked before entering the container
namespace, or there is some flaw in setting up the uidmap that the 
permission to container configuration is lost in someway. 

any ideas @jakecorrenti and feel free to move this to `crun` if needed.

Based on that assumption, this might be a fix, but I am unsure moving the call to `libcrun_container_notify_handler` around will break any other `handler`s?

```diff
From 6cced2288934ddb39b5afe7e177eda60620a2753 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Qiyu Yan <yanqiyu@fedoraproject.org>
Date: Fri, 9 Jan 2026 21:02:08 +0000
Subject: [PATCH] Move notify handler before mounts to primary uid namespace

containers/podman#27822
---
 src/libcrun/container.c | 4 ----
 src/libcrun/linux.c     | 4 ++++
 2 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

diff --git a/src/libcrun/container.c b/src/libcrun/container.c
index 17aac4cd..05788a4a 100644
--- a/src/libcrun/container.c
+++ b/src/libcrun/container.c
@@ -1336,10 +1336,6 @@ container_init_setup (void *args, pid_t own_pid, char *notify_socket,
   if (UNLIKELY (ret < 0))
     return ret;
 
-  ret = libcrun_container_notify_handler (entrypoint_args, HANDLER_CONFIGURE_BEFORE_MOUNTS, container, rootfs, err);
-  if (UNLIKELY (ret < 0))
-    return ret;
-
   /* sync 2 and 3 are sent as part of libcrun_set_mounts.  */
   ret = libcrun_set_mounts (entrypoint_args, container, rootfs, send_sync_cb, &sync_socket, err);
   if (UNLIKELY (ret < 0))
diff --git a/src/libcrun/linux.c b/src/libcrun/linux.c
index 569f6559..c6a91fbc 100644
--- a/src/libcrun/linux.c
+++ b/src/libcrun/linux.c
@@ -5033,6 +5033,10 @@ libcrun_run_linux_container (libcrun_container_t *container, container_entrypoin
   if (UNLIKELY (ret < 0))
     return ret;
 
+  ret = libcrun_container_notify_handler (args, HANDLER_CONFIGURE_BEFORE_MOUNTS, container, container->container_def->root->path, err);
+  if (UNLIKELY (ret < 0))
+    return ret;
+
   /* If a new user namespace must be created, but there are other namespaces to join, then delay
      the userns creation after the namespaces are joined.  */
   init_status.delayed_userns_create
-- 
2.52.0
```

At least with this  change krun works here, only cost being that `.krun_config.json` is created by unmapped user, but not breaking anything. 
```
$ podman --runtime=/usr/local/bin/krun run -it --rm --uidmap 0:100000:1 fedora:latest ls -al .krun_config.json
-r--r--r--. 1 nobody nobody 11358 Jan  9 21:05 .krun_config.json

$ podman --runtime=krun run -it --rm --uidmap 0:100000:1 fedora:latest ls -al .krun_config.json 
Error: krun: create directory `/run/user/1000/crun`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

$ podman --runtime=krun run -it --rm  fedora:latest ls -al .krun_config.json 
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 11336 Jan  9 21:05 .krun_config.json

$ podman --runtime=/usr/local/binkrun run -it --rm  fedora:latest ls -al .krun_config.json
Error: no valid executable found for OCI runtime /usr/local/binkrun: invalid argument

$ podman --runtime=/usr/local/bin/krun run -it --rm  fedora:latest ls -al .krun_config.json
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 11336 Jan  9 21:06 .krun_config.json


```
